title: File articles of reincorporation
description: Re-state a company under the current Companies Act of Barbados.
summary: Some Barbadian companies were originally set up under the older Companies Act. If your company needs to be brought under the current Act, you file Articles of Reincorporation under Section 355.5.
Reincorporation is not a fresh start. The company keeps the same company number and continues its existing contracts, debts and assets. What changes is the legal framework it sits under.

Articles of Reincorporation set out the modern equivalents of the old company’s constitution – the maximum number of shares, restrictions on share issues or transfers, the size of the board, and any limits on what the company can do.
Most companies file Articles of Reincorporation through their attorney-at-law, alongside an updated set of by-laws and a resolution of directors approving the change.

items: the current name and company number
the maximum number of shares the company can issue
any restrictions on issuing or transferring shares
the minimum and maximum number of directors
any restrictions on the business the company can do

items.name: Resolution of directors approving the reincorporation
Notice of registered office and directors
Photo ID for the signing officer
Proof of payment of the filing fee

fees: Filing fees are set by CAIPO. Confirm before you submit.
how_long: A decision usually takes 4 to 6 weeks.

options.heading: Through an attorney-at-law
content: A Barbadian attorney-at-law will file the articles with CAIPO.

contact.agency: Corporate Affairs and Intellectual Property Office (CAIPO)
contact.address:
Baobab Tower, Ground Floor
Warrens
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Filing fees are set by CAIPO’ – we have not named a figure because the form does not include one.
‘A decision usually takes 4 to 6 weeks’ is an estimate.
Document list (resolution, notice of registered office and directors, updated by-laws) is based on common CAIPO practice.
